questions.question: To help reduce the price of oil, new supplies are needed. However, objectors oppose exploration of new sites because of the environmental damage it may cause. Why is this statement an example of the basic economic problem? A Oil is a limited resource., B Oil is expensive., C The exploration involves demand and supply., D There are external costs involved in production.
questions.text: To help reduce the price of oil, new supplies are needed. However, objectors oppose exploration of new sites because of the environmental damage it may cause. Why is this statement an example of the basic economic problem? A Oil is a limited resource., B Oil is expensive., C The exploration involves demand and supply., D There are external costs involved in production.
options: A Oil is a limited resource.
C The exploration involves demand and supply.
D There are external costs involved in production.

questions.question: Which diagram shows an opportunity cost by movement from point X to point Y? O A capital goods consumer goods X Y O B capital goods consumer goods X Y O C capital goods consumer goods Y X O D capital goods consumer goods Y X
questions.text: Which diagram shows an opportunity cost by movement from point X to point Y? O A capital goods consumer goods X Y O B capital goods consumer goods X Y O C capital goods consumer goods Y X O D capital goods consumer goods Y X

questions.question: Helium is a gas that is limited in supply because it takes thousands of years to form. The US government holds 35% of the world’s supply of helium and has been selling its stocks. Helium is essential in medical scanners. It is also used for party balloons, which is a wasteful alternative use of a valuable good. Which two concepts apply to this statement? A demand and supply, government subsidy, B excess demand, resource allocation, C opportunity cost, private monopoly, D public sector, factors of production 3
questions.text: Helium is a gas that is limited in supply because it takes thousands of years to form. The US government holds 35% of the world’s supply of helium and has been selling its stocks. Helium is essential in medical scanners. It is also used for party balloons, which is a wasteful alternative use of a valuable good. Which two concepts apply to this statement? A demand and supply, government subsidy, B excess demand, resource allocation, C opportunity cost, private monopoly, D public sector, factors of production 3
options: A demand and supply, government subsidy
D public sector, factors of production 3

questions.question: What is an example of a public good? A healthcare, B street lights, C unemployment benefits, D vaccinations
questions.text: What is an example of a public good? A healthcare, B street lights, C unemployment benefits, D vaccinations

questions.question: Why does a government provide certain goods and services in a mixed economic system? A The government always provides goods more cheaply than private firms., B The government considers only private costs and benefits., C The government provides goods to prevent the development of monopolies., D The government provides public goods because private firms cannot charge for them.
questions.text: Why does a government provide certain goods and services in a mixed economic system? A The government always provides goods more cheaply than private firms., B The government considers only private costs and benefits., C The government provides goods to prevent the development of monopolies., D The government provides public goods because private firms cannot charge for them.
options: A The government always provides goods more cheaply than private firms.
B The government considers only private costs and benefits.
C The government provides goods to prevent the development of monopolies.
D The government provides public goods because private firms cannot charge for them.

questions.question: What is a microeconomic decision? A a central bank reducing interest rates, B a gas firm reducing its prices, C a government increasing income tax, D a government increasing the national minimum wage
questions.text: What is a microeconomic decision? A a central bank reducing interest rates, B a gas firm reducing its prices, C a government increasing income tax, D a government increasing the national minimum wage
options: A a central bank reducing interest rates
B a gas firm reducing its prices
C a government increasing income tax
D a government increasing the national minimum wage

questions.question: The diagram shows a shift in the supply curve for New Zealand’s airlines from S 1 to S 2 . S 1 S 2 O price quantity What is the cause of this shift? A a decrease in the costs of New Zealand’s airlines, B a decrease in the number of people wanting to fly to New Zealand, C an increase in the price of train and bus travel in New Zealand, D an increase in the tax on air travel in New Zealand 4

options: A a decrease in the costs of New Zealand’s airlines
B a decrease in the number of people wanting to fly to New Zealand
C an increase in the price of train and bus travel in New Zealand

questions.question: What  is  an  advantage  of  labour-intensive  production  and  what  is  an  advantage  of capital-intensive production? labour-intensive capital-intensive A each item can be individually made average costs of production are low, B keeps unemployment down fixed costs are high, C workers have pride in the product machinery may break down, D workers have to work hard there is a ready market for the product 5

questions.question: The market equilibrium wage rate is $10 per hour. What explains why the wage rate actually paid is $12 per hour? A Employers are competing for labour., B The government sets a minimum wage of $12 per hour., C There is a surplus of skilled labour., D Workers have received unpaid training.
questions.text: The market equilibrium wage rate is $10 per hour. What explains why the wage rate actually paid is $12 per hour? A Employers are competing for labour., B The government sets a minimum wage of $12 per hour., C There is a surplus of skilled labour., D Workers have received unpaid training.
options: A Employers are competing for labour.
B The government sets a minimum wage of $12 per hour.
C There is a surplus of skilled labour.
D Workers have received unpaid training.

questions.question: A firm imports 20 high quality bicycles into the US at a cost of $3000 each. It sells them all, making a profit of $1000 per bicycle. What is the firm’s average revenue? A $1000 B  $2000 C  $3000 D  $4000

questions.question: Why might measures to reduce unemployment also make inflation rise? A They encourage cheap imports., B They increase demand., C They reduce firms’ costs., D They require wages to fall. 7
questions.text: Why might measures to reduce unemployment also make inflation rise? A They encourage cheap imports., B They increase demand., C They reduce firms’ costs., D They require wages to fall. 7
options: D They require wages to fall. 7

questions.question: Which change affecting an economy’s labour market promotes economic growth? A increasing trade union strike action, B lowering the age of retirement, C reducing training of workers, D removing immigration controls
questions.text: Which change affecting an economy’s labour market promotes economic growth? A increasing trade union strike action, B lowering the age of retirement, C reducing training of workers, D removing immigration controls
options: A increasing trade union strike action
B lowering the age of retirement

questions.question: Which statement relating to supply-side policies is correct? A They aim to increase aggregate demand., B They aim to increase the long-run capacity of the economy., C They aim to reduce cyclical unemployment., D They aim to work in the short term.
questions.text: Which statement relating to supply-side policies is correct? A They aim to increase aggregate demand., B They aim to increase the long-run capacity of the economy., C They aim to reduce cyclical unemployment., D They aim to work in the short term.
options: A They aim to increase aggregate demand.
B They aim to increase the long-run capacity of the economy.
C They aim to reduce cyclical unemployment.
D They aim to work in the short term.

questions.question: Malaysia and Indonesia specialise in the production of palm oil. What is a disadvantage of specialisation? A concentration of labour in the activity in which it is most productive, B creation of jobs to meet world demand, C reliance on trade with other countries for other goods, D rising palm oil prices
questions.text: Malaysia and Indonesia specialise in the production of palm oil. What is a disadvantage of specialisation? A concentration of labour in the activity in which it is most productive, B creation of jobs to meet world demand, C reliance on trade with other countries for other goods, D rising palm oil prices
options: A concentration of labour in the activity in which it is most productive
B creation of jobs to meet world demand
C reliance on trade with other countries for other goods

questions.question: The diagram shows China’s trade with Brazil for two years. 012345 China’s exports China’s imports year 1 year 2 key $ billion What happened to China’s trade balance with Brazil between year 1 and year 2? A It experienced a falling surplus., B It experienced a rising deficit., C It moved from deficit to surplus., D It moved from surplus to deficit. 10

options: A It experienced a falling surplus.
B It experienced a rising deficit.

questions.question: What is a reason for trade protection? A to reduce a deficit on the current account of the balance of payments, B to reduce a surplus on the current account of the balance of payments, C to reduce economic growth, D to reduce inflation

options: A to reduce a deficit on the current account of the balance of payments
B to reduce a surplus on the current account of the balance of payments
